questions.question: What is crypto agility?

options.text: Ability to easily swap cryptographic algorithms

questions.explanation: Crypto agility enables algorithm replacement without major system changes.

questions.question: What are the five principles of crypto agility?
questions.explanation: These five principles enable crypto-agile system design.

questions.question: What enables algorithm negotiation?
questions.explanation: Negotiation allows peers to agree on mutually supported algorithms.

questions.question: Why is algorithm tagging important?

options.text: To identify which algorithm encrypted/signed data

questions.explanation: Tags enable correct algorithm selection during decryption/verification.

questions.question: What should systems test for crypto agility?
questions.explanation: Testing validates the system can handle algorithm changes.

questions.question: What is a provider interface pattern?
questions.explanation: Provider interfaces abstract algorithms behind common APIs.

questions.question: The SIKE break demonstrated why crypto agility is essential.
questions.explanation: When SIKE was broken, agile systems could switch to alternatives quickly.
